content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 607 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

March 1, 1977


Honorable David A. Roberti
Member of the Senate
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Senator Roberti:

     This is in response to your inquiry of January 19,
1977, with respect to Executive Order B-22-76.  I will address
each of your questions in the order they were presented in
the letter.

1.     The only records we retain which are under the
       jurisdiction of Executive Order B-22-76 are the
       reports and statements required by Government Code
       Section 81005.  With respect to those reports and
       statements, Government Code Section 81008 requires
       that each one be "open for public inspection and
       reproduction during regular business hours...."

2.     I am enclosing a copy of the statement we have
       developed to attach to forms used to collect in-
       formation from individuals.

3.     We have assigned a full-time Documents Examiner--
       and additional staff during peak filing periods--
       to insure that our files are maintained in an
       accurate, complete and timely manner.  The Docu-
       ments Examiner has read Executive Order B-22-76
       and fully understands her responsibilities.

4.     Since, pursuant to Government Code Section 81008,
       all of the relevant records are open to public
       inspection, transfer of such records under the
       Executive Order is not a matter of concern.

5.     There has not been an increase in the number of
       requests to see files since the Executive Order
       went into effect.

6.     We have not received any requests to amend files
       pursuant to the Executive Order.


TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5663) • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5903) • ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6641

Honorable David A. Roberti
March 1, 1977
Page Two


7.    This question is not applicable in light of our
      answer in item 6 above.

8.    The Executive Order has not had a significant
      impact on our agency.

9.    Our workload has not increased as a result of the
      Executive Order.

10.   We have not received questions from the public
      regarding the Executive Order.

11.   We have not changed our record keeping procedures
      as a result of the Executive Order.

      I hope the answers above are responsive to your
questions.  Please contact me if you wish additional information.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        Michael Bennett
                                        Executive Director

Attachment

SACRAMENTO OFFICE

SACRAMENTO, CA 95814

HOLLYWOOD, CA 90028

CHAIRMAN
JOINT COMMITTEE ON
THE REVISION OF
PENAL CODE

STATE SENATOR

SUBCOMMITTEE ON SMALL
BUSINESS ENTERPRISES

DAVID A. ROBERTI

COMMITTEES

MAJORITY FLOOR LEADER

BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS
(VICE CHAIRMAN)
JUDICIARY
REVENUE AND TAXATION

California Legislature
TWENTY-THIRD DISTRICT
LOS ANGELES COUNTY

REPLY TO:
☐ DISTRICT OFFICE
☒ SACRAMENTO OFFICE

January 19, 1977

Michael Bennett, Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Bennett:

On September 30, 1976, an Executive Order was issued to give citizens access
to files maintained on them by State agencies. I would appreciate knowing
what action your office has taken to comply with that order.

Under the General Agency Requirements of the Executive Order, each agency is
required to maintain only that information which is "relevant and necessary"
to accomplish a purpose of the agency required or authorized by statute. What
action has your agency taken to insure that the information maintained in your
records is "relevant and necessary"?

Under the Executive Order, each agency is required to attach to any form used
to collect information from individuals, a statement containing certain information specified in Section 2, Subsection b of the Executive Order. I would
appreciate a copy of the statement used by your agency in your agency forms
to comply with this section.

The Executive Order requires all files to be maintained with accuracy, relevancy,
timeliness, and completeness. What steps has your agency taken to insure that
your files comply with this requirement? The Executive Order requires that no
information in a file be transferred outside the agency or to another agency
unless the transfer is compatible with the purpose for which it was collected.
How have you determined when such transfers out of your agency are compatible
with the purpose for which such records were collected?

Since the Executive Order has gone into effect, has there been any increase in
the number of requests by individuals to see the files maintained by your agency
on them? Have you received any requests to amend files pursuant to the Executive

[illegible]

Michael Bennett
Page 2
January 19, 1977


Order? Has your agency refused to amend any file after a request has been made
for amendment? In your estimation has the Executive Order had a significant
impact on your agency? Has your workload increased? Have you received a large
number of questions regarding the Executive Order from the public? Have you
changed your record keeping procedures?

I appreciate your cooperation helping me understand the impact of this Executive
Order on State government.

Sincerely,

David A. Roberti

DAVID A. ROBERTI

DAR:dhc

sections.question:
s.
Please contact me it you wish additional information.
sections.conclusion: Michael Bennett, Executive Director, provided point-by-point responses: (1) FPPC retains only reports and statements required by Government Code Section 81003, which must remain open for public inspection; (2) enclosed a copy of the statement attached to forms; (3) assigned a full-time Documents Examiner to maintain accurate, complete, and timely files; (4) record transfers under the Executive Order are not a concern since all relevant records are public; (5) no increase in requests to view files; (6) no requests to amend files received; (7) not applicable; (8) Executive Order has not had significant impact; (9) workload has not increased; (10) no public questions received; (11) no changes to record keeping procedures.
sections.question_synthetic: Senator Roberti inquired about the FPPC's compliance with Executive Order B-22-76, requesting information on records jurisdiction, form statements, file maintenance procedures, information transfer compatibility determinations, and the order's operational impact on the agency.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed full compliance with Executive Order B-22-76, stating that records are limited to those required by statute and are open to public inspection, a Documents Examiner has been assigned to ensure file quality, and the order has had no significant operational impact on the agency.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR document with some formatting issues but readable. Includes both the incoming request (dated January 19, 1977) and the FPPC response (dated March 1, 1977). No distinct 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' section headers, but questions and answers are clearly demarcated by numbering. Response addresses each question sequentially.

embedding.summary: On March 1, 1977, the FPPC Executive Director responded to a detailed inquiry from Senator David A. Roberti regarding the Commission's compliance with Executive Order B-22-76, addressing 11 specific questions about records management, information collection, file maintenance, and the order's agency impact.
